questions.question: Recently, which organization released the “Trends in World Military Expenditure, 2023 Report?
questions.explanation: The “Trends in World Military Expenditure, 2023 Report” by SIPRI, established in 1966, highlights global military spending. India ranked fourth in military spending at 83.6 billion after the US, China, and Russia. NATO states’ expenditure totaled 1341 billion, comprising 55% of global spending. The report attributes the record-high global military expenditure of $2443 billion to Russia’s Ukraine invasion and geopolitical tensions. SIPRI focuses on conflict research, armaments, arms control, and disarmament.

questions.question: Recently, which country hosted the ‘Sustainable Finance for Tiger Landscapes Conference’?
questions.explanation: On Earth Day 2024, Bhutan leads the Sustainable Finance for Tiger Landscapes Conference, striving to raise $1 billion in a decade to safeguard crucial tiger habitats. Co-hosted by Bhutan’s Queen Jetsun Pema Wangchuck, the event, in collaboration with the Royal Government of Bhutan and the Tiger Conservation Coalition, highlights sustainable finance, the UN’s Global Biodiversity Framework, and essential public-private partnerships for tiger conservation.

questions.question: Voyager 1 Spacecraft, recently seen in the news, is a space probe launched by which space organization?
questions.explanation: NASA’s Voyager 1 probe, launched in 1977, is transmitting vital data after a period of malfunction, the agency announced. The spacecraft’s mission aims to explore the outer Solar System and beyond, conducting flybys of Jupiter and Saturn, discovering new moons and rings. It became the first human-made object to exit the Solar System in 2012, carrying a golden record depicting life on Earth. Voyager 1 is expected to remain operational until at least 2025, currently 13.8 billion miles away from the sun.

questions.question: Which Indian Naval Sailing Vessel recently marked a historic transoceanic expedition of nearly 2 months by two women officers of the Indian Navy?
questions.explanation: The Indian Naval Sailing Vessel INSV Tarini completed a significant transoceanic journey, led by two female officers, culminating in its return to Goa after nearly two months. During the expedition, the crew engaged with Mauritian officials and collaborated with the Mauritius Coast Guard, bolstering bilateral relations. This achievement underscores the Indian Navy’s commitment to gender equality and women’s empowerment in maritime roles. Next, the crew is gearing up for Sagar Parikrama-IV, a global circumnavigation expedition set for September 2024 aboard INSV Tarini.

questions.question: Prabowo Subianto has been appointed as the new President of which country?
questions.explanation: Prabowo Subianto, formerly Indonesia’s Defense Minister, has been appointed as country’s new President, succeeding Joko Widodo. Indonesia’s presidential term spans five years with the possibility of re-election. Subianto’s appointment marks a significant political transition, highlighting the democratic processes in Indonesia. His background in defense affairs signals potential shifts in national security policies under his leadership.

questions.question: Which country recently passed Foreign Aid Bill to provide military aid to Ukraine?
questions.explanation: President Joe Biden has signed a 95 billion military aid package, featuring 61 billion for Ukraine, 26 billion for Israel, and 8 billion for the Indo-Pacific. Notably, the bill includes provisions targeting TikTok, giving parent company ByteDance nine months to sell the app before it’s banned in the US. Months of negotiation, lobbying by Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, and a split in the House Republican conference preceded the signing, posing challenges for House Speaker Mike Johnson’s leadership due to conservative opposition to increased aid for Ukraine.

questions.question: Recently, 26th World Energy Congress is organized in which country?
questions.explanation: A Ministerial Round Table Conference convened on April 24, 2024, during the 26th World Energy Congress in Rotterdam, Netherlands. Discussions centered on the transformative impact of the COP28 UN Climate Change Conference in Dubai. The Congress, themed ‘Redesigning Energy for People and Planet’, aimed to shape global energy transitions amidst unpredictability.

questions.question: Recently, India’s first multipurpose Green Hydrogen Pilot Project was inaugurated in which state?
questions.explanation: SJVN Limited inaugurated India’s first multipurpose green hydrogen pilot project at the Nathpa Jhakri Hydro Power Station in Himachal Pradesh. Operating for eight hours daily, it aims to produce 14 kg of green hydrogen, with surplus utilized to generate electricity through a 25 kW fuel cell. The project will also produce high-velocity oxygen fuel coating material for NJHPS. Its 20 Nm/hour alkaline electrolyzer is powered by renewable energy from a 1.31 MW solar power plant in Shimla.

questions.question: Project Nilgiri Tahr, recently seen in the news, is an initiative launched by which state government?
questions.explanation: The International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) will join the three-day synchronised Nilgiri Tahr census as a third-party observer. This census, starting April 29, is the first-ever synchronised effort to estimate the Nilgiri Tahr population. Collaborators include WWF-India, WII, and AIWC. Project Nilgiri Tahr, launched by the Tamil Nadu government, spans 2022-2027, aiming to safeguard the species and ensure its survival.

questions.question: Bima Vistaar, recently seen in the news, is launched by which organization?

options.label: A
options.text: Small Industries Development Bank Of India

options.label: C
options.text: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

answer.label: C
answer.text: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

questions.explanation: Recently, the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) launched Bima Vistaar at a CEO summit, priced at Rs 1,500 per policy. This comprehensive product combines life, health, personal accident, and property insurances, targeting rural areas. Sum assured for life, accident, and property is Rs 2 lakh each, with health cover offering Rs 500 for 10 days, max Rs 5,000 without documents. Agents earn 10% commission, facilitating wider distribution via Bima Vahak, aiming to increase insurance penetration.
